step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes a complete machine learning lifecycle. It details (1) Data Scientists utilizing SageMaker Notebooks, (2) custom Docker images stored in ECR, (3) SageMaker for training and HPO, (4) S3 for datasets and model artifacts, (5) SageMaker endpoints for inference, (6) Lambda functions to enrich predictions, (7) production application workloads running on EC2, and (8) a future migration plan to ECS for endpoint serving. Separate nodes are allocated for SageMaker's distinct phases: Notebooks, Training, and Endpoints.

graph.name: ContentWise ML Training and Real-Time Recommendation Pipeline
graph.notes: Custom ML pipeline powered by SageMaker. Reduced prototyping and deployment lifecycle from one month to under a week. Leverages custom Docker images in ECR and real-time enrichment via Lambda.

nodes.name: Data Scientist
nodes.notes: Builds, optimizes, and orchestrates ML model workflows.

nodes.name: SageMaker Notebooks
nodes.notes: Interactive environment used to prototype models and orchestrate training jobs.

nodes.name: Elastic Container Registry
nodes.notes: Stores custom SageMaker container images containing proprietary libraries.

nodes.name: S3 Data Lake
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Central repository for raw datasets and serialized ML model artifacts.

nodes.name: SageMaker Training & HPO
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Scales training and executes hyperparameter optimization.

nodes.name: Enrichment Service
nodes.notes: Enriches raw ML prediction payloads with operational context data.

nodes.name: Production Applications
nodes.notes: Hosts main digital content delivery platform.

nodes.name: ECS Cluster
nodes.notes: Target for future migration to gain finer scale and cost control over endpoints.

edges.notes: Triggers training and hyperparameter optimization tasks.
Reports training job execution status to developer.
Future state: queries ECS tasks serving predictions.
Future state: pulls deployment images from ECR.
